author: JB
text: Which approach — or do you want to review/adjust the plan first? (I'd especially flag Task 1.0's SAB-enablement question, since it touches the packaged build's CSP.)

author: JB
text: One thing worth your eye before we build: **Phase 1's Task 1.0 spike** flags that SharedArrayBuffer in the Electron renderer needs cross-origin isolation (COOP/COEP) or a Chromium switch — which touches the packaged build's CSP. If that's a known-OK area for you, great; if not, it's the one real risk in the plan.

author: JB
text: How do you want to proceed — pivot to the cheap wins (uncap + camera smoothing) and re-measure, or dig into the backend contention first? I can also have you do one quick check to confirm the cull theory: tell me the **on-screen creature count** (HUD) during a stutter — if it's small, the cull explanation is locked.

author: JB
text: we are alrady uncapped. Remember, its triggered by high population, so what else could it be? Are you sure about your assesment? do you want to spike some of the other changes anyway and I visually verify?

author: JB
text: Two knobs are live in this spike: `maxFPS=0` (uncapped) and the `SMOOTH_TAU` ease. Tell me how it feels — smooth / still jerky / laggy — and I'll either promote it to a proper TDD'd implementation or pivot to the backend-contention spike.

author: JB
text: nope jerky. Have you considered the viewport culling pipeline? cos  when its paused, no stutter, and as we know, that has a bug where we don't acutally update viewport with backend to pull in new crits

author: JB
text: Hold on, you seem so confidant that this is the problem " it's the per-tick cull/export + sim work that runs every tick regardless", so why are we not just fixing that?

author: JB
text: Hold on, you seem so confidant that this is the problem " it's the per-tick cull/export + sim work that runs every tick regardless", so why are we not just fixing that? Ok, list ALL the things it could be, in most likely order, and also remember we had multiple phases we were going to do with combining benefit. I have lost track of what the fuck we are doing. So, list all the hypothesis of the cause, most likely first and include all the other smoothing and improvement options we were going to do. Then, we will try one by one, with me visually verifying at each step to find and fix this.

author: JB
text: Want me to apply **B (reserve cores)** now? It's a small early-init change in the Rust engine (cap Bevy's `ComputeTaskPool` + Rayon global pool to `cores − 2`) plus a `dev:rust` rebuild, then you eyeball 1M. Uncap (A) is already live, so you can also just tell me how A alone feels first.
